content.text:
## Task: Fix [medium] UI/UX: Move/Copy prompts use developer jargon ('relative destination path')
## Step: write-tests

You are a fix agent. Resolve the following final-review finding.

Review dimension: UI/UX
Severity: medium
File: src/app/dom-builders.ts:340-360 (makeRowMenu Move/Copy handlers)
Title: Move/Copy prompts use developer jargon ('relative destination path')

Finding:
The Move and Copy actions use `window.prompt('Move to relative destination path:', entry.path)` and `window.prompt('Copy to relative destination path:', entry.path)`. 'Relative destination path' is internal/developer terminology that end users of a file browser will not understand; it also does not tell them the path is relative to the storage root or how separators work. The copy should be plain and action-oriented, consistent with the rest of the UI (e.g. 'New directory name:', 'Delete "x"?'). The prompts pre-fill `entry.path` as the default, which is a helpful hint and should stay.

Fix instructions (follow exactly; make targeted, minimal changes):
In src/app/dom-builders.ts in `makeRowMenu`, change the Move handler's prompt string from `'Move to relative destination path:'` to `'Move "' + entry.name + '" to:'` and the Copy handler's prompt string from `'Copy to relative destination path:'` to `'Copy "' + entry.name + '" to:'`. Keep the second argument (`entry.path`) as the default value and the rest of the handler (null-check, `normalizeRelativePath`, API call, `rerender()`) unchanged. Update any toolbar-handlers/dom-builders test that asserts the literal old prompt string to expect the new one.

## Review Feedback History (please address all items)
Attempt 1: The test changes submitted do not address the Move/Copy prompt jargon fix at all. All changes in the working tree (render-browse.test.ts, dom-builders.test.ts, render-orchestration.test.ts, start-app.test.ts) are accessibility improvements (WCAG 1.3.1 aria-hidden on decorative icons, WCAG 4.1.3 role='status' for spinner, role='menu'/role='menuitem' patterns, focus management). While these are valid improvements, they are unrelated to the prompt string fix. No tests anywhere in the suite assert the Move/Copy prompt string text — neither the old jargon strings ('Move to relative destination path:') nor the new user-friendly strings ('Move "x" to:'). The only prompt string assertion in the entire test suite is for 'New directory name:' at render-browse.test.ts:1098. The fix instructions require updating tests that assert the old prompt strings, but since none exist, the coverage gap needs to be filled with new tests.
